evals.prompt: We want to show user names and avatars in comments, cursors, and avatar stacks. How do we add user information so users aren't anonymous?
evals.expected_output: Recommend add-user-information. Explain authenticating with userInfo (name, avatar, color) and setting up resolvers for comments, notifications, cursors.
assertions: The response mentions adding user info, userInfo, or resolvers for comments/cursors
The response mentions at least one of: name, avatar, color

evals.prompt: We want AI to collaborate like a human in our Liveblocks app — editing content and taking actions. How do we set up AI as a collaborator?
evals.expected_output: Recommend ai-as-a-collaborator. Explain agentic workflows so AI can collaborate inside the app.
assertions: The response mentions AI as a collaborator or agentic workflows
The response points to Liveblocks docs or guidance for enabling this

evals.prompt: We need an AI chat with RAG, tool calling, and custom UI components in our app. Not the workflow-based collaborator — the chat/copilot kind. How do we set this up with Liveblocks?
evals.expected_output: Recommend ai-chats-with-tools-knowledge-components. Explain Liveblocks AI Copilots: AiChat, RAG, knowledge, tools, custom components.
assertions: The response mentions AI chat, Copilot, or chat with tools/knowledge
The response distinguishes from or does not confuse with workflow-based AI collaborator

evals.prompt: I need to pass custom headers when Liveblocks fetches the auth token, and I want to avoid automatic reconnection when the token is invalid. Can I use a callback instead of a string for authEndpoint?
evals.expected_output: Recommend auth-endpoint-callback. Explain using a callback for authEndpoint for custom fetch logic, custom headers, and controlling reconnection.
assertions: The response mentions using a callback for authEndpoint or custom fetch
The response mentions custom headers or preventing reconnection when token is invalid

evals.prompt: We want a simple auth method for Liveblocks — just identify the user by ID and basic info. What's the simpler alternative to ID tokens?
evals.expected_output: Recommend authenticating-with-access-tokens. Explain identifyUser with prepareSession for simpler permissions.
assertions: The response mentions access tokens or prepareSession/identifyUser for simple auth
The response indicates this is simpler than or an alternative to ID tokens

evals.prompt: We need complex permissions per room and user. What's the recommended way to authenticate with Liveblocks?
evals.expected_output: Recommend authenticating-with-id-tokens. Explain ID tokens as the recommended method for complex permissions.
assertions: The response recommends ID tokens for Liveblocks authentication
The response mentions identifyUser or complex permissions

evals.prompt: Our rooms are hitting the user limit. How can we avoid filling up with inactive users?
evals.expected_output: Recommend avoid-hitting-user-limit-in-rooms. Explain backgroundKeepAliveTimeout on LiveblocksProvider to disconnect inactive users after a timeout.
assertions: The response mentions backgroundKeepAliveTimeout or disconnecting inactive users
The response mentions user limit or room capacity

evals.prompt: We have multiple workspaces/tenants. How do we compartmentalize rooms, notifications, and comment threads per organization in Liveblocks?
evals.expected_output: Recommend compartmentalize-resources-with-organizations. Explain setting organizationId on rooms, notifications, and filtering when listing.
assertions: The response mentions organizationId or organizations
The response mentions compartmentalizing rooms or workspaces

evals.prompt: We need a fully custom comment composer that doesn't use the default one. How do we build our own with Liveblocks?
evals.expected_output: Recommend create-custom-comment-composer. Explain useComposer and Composer.Form primitives for advanced custom composers.
assertions: The response mentions useComposer or Composer.Form or custom comment composer
The response points to primitives or building your own composer

evals.prompt: We're building a Figma-like or custom multiplayer canvas with conflict-free state. How do we use Liveblocks for custom realtime multiplayer beyond text editors?
evals.expected_output: Recommend create-custom-realtime-multiplayer. Explain Liveblocks Storage with LiveList, LiveMap, LiveObject for custom multiplayer apps.
assertions: The response mentions Liveblocks Storage or LiveList/LiveMap/LiveObject
The response mentions custom multiplayer or sync engine

evals.prompt: We want a custom toolbar for our Tiptap or Lexical editor that matches our design — static or floating. How do we do this with Liveblocks?
evals.expected_output: Recommend create-custom-text-editor-toolbar. Explain Toolbar and FloatingToolbar from Liveblocks with custom buttons and styles.
assertions: The response mentions Toolbar or FloatingToolbar for Tiptap/Lexical
The response mentions Liveblocks react-tiptap or custom toolbar

evals.prompt: Should we create Liveblocks rooms manually in production or let them be created on first connection?
evals.expected_output: Recommend create-rooms-manually. Explain creating rooms yourself in production with getOrCreateRoom, permissions, metadata, organization.
assertions: The response recommends creating rooms yourself in production
The response mentions getOrCreateRoom or permissions/metadata

evals.prompt: We want to customize how comments look inside threads — avatars, author, body. Can we use slots or override parts of the Thread component?
evals.expected_output: Recommend customize-thread-components. Explain using Thread component slots (e.g. Comment, avatar, author) to customize comment UI.
assertions: The response mentions customizing Thread or comment components with slots
The response mentions components or slots for avatar, author, or comment body

evals.prompt: Our app supports dark mode. How do we get dark mode styles for Liveblocks UI components?
evals.expected_output: Recommend dark-mode-styles. Explain importing @liveblocks/react-ui styles and dark theme (media-query or attributes).
assertions: The response mentions importing Liveblocks dark mode CSS
The response mentions prefers-color-scheme or dark theme option

evals.prompt: We want to develop and run E2E tests without hitting Liveblocks production. How do we develop and test locally?
evals.expected_output: Recommend develop-and-test-locally. Explain the Liveblocks dev server (npx liveblocks dev), local baseUrl, and CI/E2E testing.
assertions: The response mentions the Liveblocks dev server or local development
The response mentions testing without production or baseUrl

evals.prompt: How can we inspect Liveblocks Storage, Yjs state, presence, and connected users in the browser?
evals.expected_output: Recommend devtools-extension. Explain the Liveblocks DevTools browser extension for Chrome, Firefox, Edge.
assertions: The response mentions Liveblocks DevTools or browser extension
The response mentions inspecting Storage, presence, or connected users

evals.prompt: We need to change button labels and tooltips in Liveblocks components (e.g. 'Reply' to 'Comment') and support other languages. How?
evals.expected_output: Recommend edit-component-text-strings. Explain LiveblocksUiConfig overrides for locale and string overrides (USER_UNKNOWN, etc.).
assertions: The response mentions overriding strings or locale for Liveblocks components
The response mentions LiveblocksUiConfig or overrides

evals.prompt: Users sometimes can't join the room — auth failure, no permission, room full, or room ID changed. How do we handle these connection errors in Liveblocks?
evals.expected_output: Recommend handling-connection-errors. Explain useErrorListener and ROOM_CONNECTION_ERROR with codes for auth, permission, full room, room ID change.
assertions: The response mentions useErrorListener or handling connection errors
The response mentions ROOM_CONNECTION_ERROR or error codes (e.g. 4001, 4005, 4006)

evals.prompt: What do we do when a user tries to join a Liveblocks room that's already full?
evals.expected_output: Recommend handling-full-rooms. Explain useErrorListener and checking for code 4005 (room full), then redirect or show error UI.
assertions: The response mentions handling full room or code 4005
The response mentions useErrorListener

evals.prompt: Our Liveblocks Thread component or useCreateThread sometimes throws. How do we catch and handle these errors?
evals.expected_output: Recommend handling-hook-and-component-errors. Explain useErrorListener for CREATE_THREAD_ERROR and other hook/component errors.
assertions: The response mentions useErrorListener for thread or component errors
The response mentions CREATE_THREAD_ERROR or catching errors from hooks/components

evals.prompt: Users with poor network see disconnections. How do we show fallbacks and messages when the Liveblocks connection is lost or restored?
evals.expected_output: Recommend handling-unstable-connections. Explain useLostConnectionListener for 'lost', 'restored', 'failed' and showing UI (e.g. toasts).
assertions: The response mentions useLostConnectionListener or handling unstable connection
The response mentions lost, restored, or failed connection states

evals.prompt: We have an SPA and need to log the user out of Liveblocks and reauthenticate without a full page refresh. How?
evals.expected_output: Recommend log-out-of-liveblocks. Explain client.logout() and getting the client via useClient for SPA reauth.
assertions: The response mentions client.logout() or logging out of Liveblocks
The response mentions useClient or reauthenticating without refresh

evals.prompt: We need several Tiptap or BlockNote editors on the same page, each synced with Liveblocks. How do we do multiple text editors?
evals.expected_output: Recommend multiple-text-editors. Explain the field prop to give each editor an ID, and optionally storing field IDs in Storage.
assertions: The response mentions the field prop or multiple editors on one page
The response mentions Tiptap or BlockNote with Liveblocks

evals.prompt: We want the text editor to load instantly from cache and sync when Liveblocks connects, without a loading spinner. How do we enable offline support?
evals.expected_output: Recommend offline-support-in-text-editors. Explain offlineSupport_experimental for Yjs/Tiptap/BlockNote and structuring the app so the editor loads from local state first.
assertions: The response mentions offline support or loading without spinner
The response mentions offlineSupport_experimental or local/cache first

evals.prompt: We want to style Liveblocks default components (comments, buttons) to match our app. Can we override colors and spacing?
evals.expected_output: Recommend override-css-variables. Explain .lb-root and CSS variables (--lb-accent, --lb-spacing, --lb-radius) for styling Liveblocks components.
assertions: The response mentions CSS variables or .lb-root for styling Liveblocks
The response mentions overriding styles (e.g. accent, spacing, radius)

evals.prompt: Presence and useOthers are updating too often and our cursor/avatar components re-render constantly. How do we make presence updates more performant?
evals.expected_output: Recommend performant-others-and-presence. Explain useOtherConnectionIds, useOthersMapped, useOther, and shallow to avoid unnecessary re-renders.
assertions: The response mentions useOthersMapped, useOther, or useOtherConnectionIds
The response mentions shallow or reducing re-renders from presence

evals.prompt: useStorage is causing too many re-renders when any part of storage changes. How do we make storage reads more performant?
evals.expected_output: Recommend performant-storage-with-selectors. Explain useStorage with selectors (e.g. (root) => root.animals) and shallow to subscribe only to needed slice.
assertions: The response mentions useStorage with selectors or selector functions
The response mentions shallow or reducing re-renders

evals.prompt: Users sometimes close the tab before Liveblocks has synced their changes. How do we warn them and prevent losing unsaved data?
evals.expected_output: Recommend prevent-unsaved-changes-being-lost. Explain preventUnsavedChanges on LiveblocksProvider to show a dialog and block tab close when there are unsynced changes.
assertions: The response mentions preventUnsavedChanges or preventing loss of unsaved changes
The response mentions LiveblocksProvider or dialog when closing tab

evals.prompt: We want to build our own comment UI using our design system but still need comment body, mentions, links. Can we use low-level pieces from Liveblocks?
evals.expected_output: Recommend primitive-component-parts. Explain primitives (e.g. Comment.Body) from @liveblocks/react-ui/primitives for headless, unstyled building blocks.
assertions: The response mentions primitives or primitive components
The response mentions Comment.Body or building custom UI with Liveblocks pieces

evals.prompt: How do we remove or hide the 'Powered by Liveblocks' badge from our app?
evals.expected_output: Recommend remove-liveblocks-branding. Explain dashboard setting to remove branding or badgeLocation on LiveblocksProvider to move it.
assertions: The response mentions removing the Liveblocks badge or branding
The response mentions dashboard settings or badgeLocation

evals.prompt: We use Liveblocks suspense hooks. How do we show error fallbacks when a hook throws (e.g. room failed to load)?
evals.expected_output: Recommend rendering-error-components. Explain ErrorBoundary (e.g. react-error-boundary) wrapping components that use Liveblocks suspense hooks.
assertions: The response mentions ErrorBoundary for error fallbacks
The response mentions suspense hooks or handling hook errors

evals.prompt: We use Liveblocks suspense hooks. How do we show loading spinners or skeletons while the room is connecting?
evals.expected_output: Recommend rendering-loading-components. Explain ClientSideSuspense with fallback for loading state, and using multiple boundaries per section.
assertions: The response mentions ClientSideSuspense or loading fallback
The response mentions suspense hooks or loading state

evals.prompt: We want to email users when they're mentioned in a comment or have unread thread activity. How do we send comment notification emails with Liveblocks?
evals.expected_output: Recommend send-comment-notification-emails. Explain webhooks, WebhookHandler, isThreadNotificationEvent, and @liveblocks/emails (e.g. prepareThreadNotificationEmailAsReact).
assertions: The response mentions webhooks or sending emails for comment notifications
The response mentions @liveblocks/emails or thread notification events

evals.prompt: Presence and storage updates feel choppy. Can we make Liveblocks updates smoother (higher frame rate)?
evals.expected_output: Recommend smoother-realtime-updates. Explain throttle on LiveblocksProvider (default 100ms, min 16ms for ~60fps) for more frequent updates.
assertions: The response mentions throttle on LiveblocksProvider or update frequency
The response mentions 60fps or smoother updates

evals.prompt: What's the difference between importing from @liveblocks/react and @liveblocks/react/suspense? When do we use ClientSideSuspense and when do we use isLoading?
evals.expected_output: Recommend suspense-vs-regular-hooks. Explain suspense hooks with ClientSideSuspense/ErrorBoundary vs regular hooks with { error, isLoading }.
assertions: The response explains the difference between /suspense and regular hooks
The response mentions ClientSideSuspense or isLoading/error

evals.prompt: We're using Tiptap with Liveblocks. What do we need for SSR, initial content, unsaved changes, and required extensions?
evals.expected_output: Recommend tiptap-best-practices. Explain StarterKit (or Doc + Paragraph), SSR, initial content, unsaved changes handling, and Liveblocks integration.
assertions: The response mentions StarterKit or Doc/Paragraph for Tiptap
The response mentions at least one of: SSR, initial content, unsaved changes, Liveblocks

evals.prompt: We want to send a notification to a user's Liveblocks inbox for something that isn't a comment — e.g. someone uploaded a file. How do we trigger custom notifications?
evals.expected_output: Recommend trigger-custom-notifications. Explain triggerInboxNotification with a custom kind (e.g. $fileUploaded) and userId, subjectId, activityData.
assertions: The response mentions triggerInboxNotification or custom inbox notifications
The response mentions custom kind (e.g. $ prefix) or non-comment notifications

evals.prompt: How do we get TypeScript types for Liveblocks presence, storage, user info, and room metadata across our app?
evals.expected_output: Recommend type-liveblocks-correctly. Explain liveblocks.config.ts and the Liveblocks global interface (Presence, Storage, UserMeta, etc.) and create-liveblocks-app --init.
assertions: The response mentions liveblocks.config.ts or typing Liveblocks
The response mentions Presence, Storage, UserMeta or global types

evals.prompt: Our app has one document per URL (e.g. /doc/abc123). How should we set the Liveblocks room ID — use the document ID from the URL?
evals.expected_output: Recommend url-params-in-room-id. Explain using URL params (e.g. slug or doc id) as the room ID and optionally document title in the URL.
assertions: The response recommends using URL params as room ID
The response mentions document or room ID from URL

evals.prompt: We need a relative timestamp ('5 minutes ago') and an emoji picker in our Liveblocks UI. Does Liveblocks provide these?
evals.expected_output: Recommend utility-components. Explain Timestamp, Duration, EmojiPicker (and other utilities) from Liveblocks react-ui primitives.
assertions: The response mentions Timestamp, Duration, EmojiPicker or utility components
The response mentions @liveblocks/react-ui primitives or utilities

evals.prompt: We're using Yjs with Liveblocks (not Tiptap/BlockNote/Lexical). How do we avoid duplicate Yjs imports, use subdocuments, and get the provider with getYjsProviderForRoom?
evals.expected_output: Recommend yjs-best-practices. Explain fixing duplicate imports, YKeyValue for maps, subdocuments when appropriate, V2 encoding, getYjsProviderForRoom. Note not relevant if using Tiptap/BlockNote/Lexical.
assertions: The response mentions Yjs with Liveblocks or getYjsProviderForRoom
The response mentions at least one of: duplicate imports, YKeyValue, subdocuments, V2 encoding

evals.prompt: Tooltips and dropdowns from Liveblocks components are rendering behind our app's modals. How do we fix z-index issues with portaled elements?
evals.expected_output: Recommend z-index-issues. Explain that Liveblocks portaled elements use .lb-portal; set z-index on the inner element or .lb-tooltip/.lb-dropdown (not on the container).
assertions: The response mentions z-index or portaled elements
The response mentions .lb-portal or targeting floating elements for z-index
